evidence: V developed the Three Freedoms framework for his Zo demo: Freedom 1 (Reclaiming Time), Freedom 2 (Learning & Strategy via Personas), Freedom 3 (Ownership)
V submits that AI productivity feels like a scam because it focuses on moving words around rather than making them actionable
Zo's unlock is the Gestalt - the totality of data flow and control
Both V and Kristen expressed frustration with the Resume Industrial Complex and limitations of traditional resumes
V proposed Careerspan act as high-fidelity data stream or enrichment layer for platforms like Elly.ai
V highlighted Careerspan's deep relationships with alumni associations (Emory, Cornell) as exclusive talent pool access
Kristen will introduce V to co-founder Nick to discuss technical integration and white-labeling
V and Jatin discuss strategic evolution from direct recruitment to talent sourcing API
V reiterates his goal: Building the Internet of Careers
V mentions giving space on the cap table to companies that source through Careerspan as quid pro quo
Jatin likens Careerspan to ZoomInfo for people data, which V confirms as ZoomInfo for talent
V argues the talent exists but is invisible - the 70% hidden slice
V describes a socialist view of data where collective contributions to a data pot benefit everyone
V proposed Triangle of Integrity where interview data from Ribbon triangulates with Careerspan stories to prevent BSing
Christine engaged positively with the Triangle of Integrity concept and offered team introductions
V articulated Careerspan's core premise: the resume is an insufficient, unauthenticated data structure
V discussed monetization motion targeting VCs to replace static job boards with active talent calls
